headline: Luxury Homes Are Selling in a Tough Market. That’s a Good Sign for Toll Brothers’ Earnings.
summary: Luxury home sales have increased this spring against an otherwise stagnant market.  Continued activity among well-heeled buyers should be a plus for  Toll Brothers  when it reports its second-quarter results on Tuesday.  Analysts expect that  Toll Brothers  which constructs luxury homes with an average price point around $1 million, will report earnings of $2.58 a share on roughly $2.42 billion in revenue for its second quarter ended April 30.
